rulings.Case Title: JAMES W. GAY VS. CREATIVE ALLIANCE, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILTY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Evidentiary Objections
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 25, 2014, Line 7, PLAINTIFF JAMES GAY Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Evidentiary Objections. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Court requests clarification on precise relief requested and the interrogatory numbers at issue. No motion to compel further responses is pending before the court, and to the extent that the motion seeks relief on waiver of evidentiary objections with respect to admissibility at trial, those issues are not before the court but must be brought at trial. Accordingly, the only waiver on which the law and motion court could rule would pertain to the form of the interrogatories or a waiver of any privilege in the responses. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: MORGAN TEMPLETON et al VS. ALUMA SYSTEMS CONCRETE CONSTRUCTION, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 25, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT NIBBI BROS. ASSOCIATES, INC. DBA NIBBI CONCRETE Motion For Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion denied. rulings.Case Title: KEVIN P CAVANAUGH VS. BRYAN KEMNITZER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Set Aside And Revoke Demurrer To Second Amended Comlaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 25, 2014, Line 5, PLAINTIFF KEVIN CAVANAUGH's Motion To Set Aside And Revoke Demurrer To Second Amended Complaint. Motion denied. rulings.Case Title: MINOO SULTAN VS. NEW MARINA REAL ESTATE SOLUTIONS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 25, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT ZOHRE GROTHE's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. The First Amended Complaint ("FAC") adequately pleads the elements of fraud. The FAC pleads that Defendant prepared and executed a lease in connection with the sale of the property to represent to Plaintiff that Defendant's office suite would generate over $3,000 in monthly rent (FAC, par. 36), Defendant knowingly overstated the value of the rental income from the office space in the lease (FAC, pars. 94, 96), Defendant intended for Plaintiff to rely on the lease to increase the purchase price of the property (FAC, par. 95), Plaintiff reasonably relied on Defendant's misrepresentation (FAC, par. 97), and Plaintiff was damaged by this misrepresentation (FAC, pars. 97-98). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Change Venue
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 25, 2014, Line 14, DEFENDANT WILLIAM YAO's Motion To Change Venue. Granted. (CCP sec. 397(a).) The case is transferred to Los Angeles County. Plaintiff shall pay all transfer costs. Plaintiff admits in its opposition that there was no basis for filing the complaint in San Francisco County. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: BA BOTTOM LINE, LLC VS. SAFEDOCS INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ba Bottom Line, Llc'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Appointment Of Discovery Referee
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 25, 2014, Line 8, PLAINTIFF BA BOTTOM LINE, LLC Ba Bottom Line, Llc'S Motion For Appointment Of Discovery Referee. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
